source_name: Code4rena Kinetiq Findings and Analysis Report (2025-04)
finding_title: Users Who Queue Withdrawal Before A Slashing Event Disadvantage Users Who Queue After And Eventually Leads To Loss Of Funds For Them
summary: Lets take the scenario where the HYPE to KHYPE exchange is 1 KHYPE = 1.5 KHYPE.
root_cause: withdrawal confirmation uses a queued snapshot hypeAmount instead of recalculating claim value with the current exchange rate after slashing
broken_invariants: queued withdrawals must share post-queue slashing losses consistently with remaining and later-withdrawing holders
confirmWithdrawal must not pay a stale pre-slash HYPE amount when the kHYPE/HYPE exchange rate has changed
withdrawal request snapshots must not let early queueing users extract value from users who queue after a slash
attack_preconditions: Protocol state reaches the report-described edge case or queue configuration
Relevant manager/operator/user flow is executed as described in the public finding
attacker_capability: external user or protocol participant able to interact with the affected staking/withdrawal/deposit flow
trigger_actions: execute the affected function sequence described in the report
observe accounting/state transition divergence or operation failure

impact.description: Lets take the scenario where the HYPE to KHYPE exchange is 1 KHYPE = 1.5 KHYPE.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and exploit/impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: check whether the affected function is actually reachable under current roles/pauses/configuration
check whether sponsor mitigations or operational assumptions fully eliminate the stated state transition
check whether the code version and line links match the audited commit
mitigations: A possible mitigation would be when confirming withdrawals, not to use the hypeAmount stored in the withdrawal request but to recalculate with the new ratio.
